title: astripomoea malvacea
intro: Astripomoea malvacea, also known as the Malayan mistletoe or Malayan broom, is a parasitic plant species that belongs to the family Convolvulaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory problems. Astripomoea malvacea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in traditional medicine, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
